title: Ukraine completes trials of long-range FPV drones with 100-150 km strike range
summary: Mench OSINT visual analysis of Ukrainian FPV drone trials completed on April 30 confirms a new long-range variant with claimed 100-150 km strike radius set to enter Armed Forces of Ukraine service within weeks, expanding tactical-strike depth without recourse to imported missiles.
contents:
# Ukraine completes trials of long-range FPV drones with 100-150 km strike range

## Equipment Identification
Mench OSINT analyses imagery from Ukrainian government and contractor channels confirming completion of trials for a new long-range FPV drone variant on April 30, 2026. Visible identifying features include enlarged main wing, redesigned airframe, datalink antenna placement consistent with mesh repeater architecture, and modified warhead bay supporting fragmentation or shaped-charge payloads. Ukraine state representatives state the drone offers a strike radius of 100-150 km.

## Capability Assessment
The new FPV variant approximately doubles the operational reach of fielded first-person-view loitering munitions, enabling Ukrainian forces to engage Russian rear-area logistics, command nodes and air defence systems without expending imported precision-guided munitions. Production scaling and operator training timelines suggest fielding to combat formations within weeks rather than months.

## Doctrinal Implications
Long-range FPV drones complement deeper strikes by SSU Alpha Centre and Defence Intelligence formations, creating a layered Ukrainian strike architecture: long-range (1,500+ km) for strategic targets, medium-range (100-150 km) for operational rear, and short-range FPV (5-30 km) for tactical contact. The new tier closes the previous gap between conventional artillery range and strategic strike weapons.
